caption: Histopathological changes in the kidney of animals in the control and treated groups (H&E staining, 40x), scale bar = 50 μm. C represents that the glomerular structure was normal (black arrow). There is no bleeding or hyperemia in the glomerulus. Renal tubules are normal (red arrow). Tubular cells are active, and the nucleus of tubular cells are not degenerated. S represents acute tubular damage, acidophilization of cytoplasm (black arrow), destruction of the wall of tubular cells and disintegration of epithelial cells of tubules, and interstitial nephritis (red arrow). T1 represents interstitial nephritis (black arrow), glomerular sclerosis, and increased glomerular space (space between the glomerulus and glomerular wall) (blue arrow). T2 represents extensive penetration of mononuclear inflammatory cells into the interstitial tubular and glomerular space (black arrow) and acute tubular necrosis caused by eosinophilization or acidophilization of the cytoplasm, due to the sedimentation of cytoplasmic proteins (red arrow). T3 represents that mononuclear inflammatory cells were seen in the interstitial space of the tubule (black arrow), the tubules are functional, and no acute tubular necrosis was observed (red arrow).
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not directly specified in the caption, however, it mentions histopathological changes, so it is likely done through microscopic examination of stained tissue samples.
A2: Based on the information provided in the caption, this image shows a region of the kidney.

A3: Yes, the abnormalities identified include acute tubular damage, interstitial nephritis, glomerular sclerosis, increased glomerular space, penetration of mononuclear inflammatory cells, and acute tubular necrosis.

A4: The image shows both normal and irregular features. Normal features include normal glomerular structure, no bleeding or hyperemia in glomerulus, normal renal tubules, non-degenerative nuclei of tubular cells, and functional tubules. Irregularities include acute tubular damage, interstitial nephritis, glomerular sclerosis, increased glomerular space, extensive penetration of inflammatory cells, and acute tubular necrosis.

A5: Yes, the image contains several labels. The labels "C," "S," "T1," "T2," and "T3" refer to different observations and conditions in the examined tissue. Additionally, there's a significance in the description of different abnormalities with the help of colored arrows (black, red, blue) pointing to different structures or changes in the kidney tissue.

caption: (i) Through-skull cranial window with mapped areas according to “The Mouse Brain in Stereotaxic Coordinates”48, relative to bregma. (S1BF Barrel field of the sensory cortex). Bregma shown with a white square. (ii) Average voltage traces from the S1BF area in response to paw stimulation from Control or ACh-lesioned mice. Vertical dashed line indicates stimulation onset. (iii) Maximum depolarisation amplitude, Unpaired t-test, t (117) = 0.4137, p = 0.6799. (iv) Time of maximum depolarization, Mann–Whitney test, U = 1411, p = 0.0528. (v) Maximum hyperpolarisation amplitude, Mann–Whitney test, U = 1624, p = 0.4430. All data are mean ± SEM. *p < 0.05, **p < 0.01, ***p < 0.001. NS non-significant. Scale bars 0.1% ΔR/R and 100 ms
answers: A1: The caption does not specify the imaging modality used for the image.
A2: The image depicts the brain region of a mouse, more specifically the S1BF Barrel field of the sensory cortex.
A3: The caption does not provide information indicating any abnormalities in the image.
A4: The caption does not provide information about the normality or any irregularities in the image.

A5: Yes, the image contains labels or indices such as the mapped areas according to "The Mouse Brain in Stereotaxic Coordinates", the location of bregma indicated by a white square, average voltage traces from the S1BF area, and statistical data indicating maximum depolarization and hyperpolarization amplitudes. The 'p' values from different tests are also noteworthy as they help in identifying the significance of the data shown.

caption: IFM showing localization of CagT and CagX in ΔcagV strain. Bacterial cells were harvested from BHI‐agar plate, fixed, and permeabilized with 0.2% TritonX‐100 as described in Materials and methods. NP and P stand for nonpermeabilized and permeabilized cells, respectively. Primary antibodies used in IFM are indicated. Preimmune serum (PI) was used as a negative control. Alexa fluor 488 (green color)‐conjugated secondary antibody was used for detection of the antigens. Magnification bar indicates 5 μm. Out of 340 ΔcagV cells (from three different experiments) tested, CagT specific fluorescence signals were detected in 250 cells under NP and out of 390, 310 cells showed fluorescence under P conditions. Similarly, out of 490 ΔcagV cells tested, CagX‐specific signals were detected in 355 cells under NP and out of 620, 500 cells showed fluorescence under P conditions.
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is Immunofluorescence microscopy (IFM).
A2: The caption does not specify any body region or anatomical area. It discusses bacterial cells, specifically the ΔcagV strain.
A3: The caption does not mention any abnormalities in the image.
A4: The caption does not specify whether the image shows any irregularities. It talks about the detection of specific signals in the bacterial cells, but does not label these as normal or irregular.

A5: The image uses labels specifically for nonpermeabilized and permeabilized cells (NP & P), as well as CagT and CagX specific fluorescence signals. caption: Effects of BA on vessel density. Vessels were stained with von Willebrand factor. Compared with the (A) control group, the density of the vessels was significantly reduced in (B) group II. By contrast, the density was significantly increased in groups (C) III, (D) IV and (E) V compared with group II (magnification, ×100). Group II (I/R): Subsequent to equilibration, hearts were subjected to ischemia for 30 min followed by reperfusion for 60 min with K-H solution. Groups III, IV and V (I/R + BA): Hearts were perfused similarly to group II, except that the reperfusion solution contained 20, 40 and 80 mg/kg BA, respectively. BA, baicalin; K-H, Krebs-Henseleit; I/R, ischemia-reperfusion.

caption: This is an example case of left enlarged pelvis lymph node of metastatic penis cancer patient treated with 3D MLC‐based SFRT. Very large pelvis lymph node that was perturbing to the skin was treated for a large single‐dose of 15 Gy with a 110% hotspot inside the GTV via 3D MLC‐based SFRT. For four of six treatment fields with 10‐MV beam, 5 mm bolus, a maximal dose rate of 600 MU/min was used. Gantry angles, 270° and 210°, were not used; Lower maximal dose to critical organs, left femoral head (4.3 Gy) and small bowel (1.3 Gy), was achieved. 3D, three‐dimensional; GTV, gross tumor volume; SFRT, spatially fractionated radiation therapy
answers: A1: The caption does not specify the imaging modality used.
A2: The image caption refers to the left pelvis and the region of the left femoral head, lymph nodes and small bowel.
A3: Yes, an enlargement of the left pelvis lymph node was identified in this image. It's associated with metastatic penis cancer.
A4: The image shows irregularities. The presence of a significantly enlarged lymph node perturbing the skin indicates pathology.

A5: The significant labels or indexes in this image are related to the treatment, including the GTV (Gross Tumor Volume), SFRT (spatially fractionated radiation therapy), and specific treatment parameters such as the hotspot, delivery rate, and dose to critical organs.

caption: Inhibition of MpPA14 binding to the diatom C. neogracile. Representative images showing (A) an untreated C. neogracile diatom with basal autofluorescence in the center from its cell; (B) a C. neogracile cell treated with 0.2 mg/ml fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC)-labeled MpPA14; (C) a C. neogracile cell treated with 0.5 mM l-fucose and 0.2 mg/ml FITC-labeled MpPA14; and (D) a C. neogracile cell treated with 0.2 mg/ml FITC-labeled MpPA14 and 50 mM GalNAc. All four images (A to D) are at the same scale, with the black scale bar in panel D indicating 10 μm. (E) Fluorescence levels shown by the C. neogracile cells alone and those treated with various ligands. Each bar represents the quantification of average fluorescence from 30 individual diatoms. Data bars with an asterisk underneath represent experiments where diatoms were incubated with FITC-MpPA14 before l-fucose was added.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not directly stated in the caption. However, the references to fluorescence levels suggest that the images are taken using a fluorescence microscopy.

A3: The caption does not mention any abnormalities in the images.
A4: The caption does not indicate any irregularities in C. neogracile's cell. Rather, it discusses the observable effects when the diatoms are treated with different chemicals.

caption: Image data analysis. Workflow for region of interest (ROI) based positron emission tomography (PET) image analysis. For each subject, at each time point, we co-register the PET images to the T1 weighted magnetic resonance images (MRI), which we segment using FreeSurfer to calculate the standardized uptake value ratios (SUVR) for each region of interest (ROI). Our study contains 46 subjects, 3–4 time points, and 83 regions of interest.
answers: A1: The imaging modalities represented in this image are Positron Emission Tomography (PET) and T1 weighted Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI).
A2: The caption does not provide specific information about the body region or anatomical area depicted in the image.
A3: The caption does not indicate the presence of any abnormalities in the image.
A4: The image's normality or presence of any irregularities is not specified in the caption.

A5: The image contains significant indexes such as the standardized uptake value ratios (SUVR) for each region of interest (ROI). The FreeSurfer segmentation of the T1-weighted MRI images is also noteworthy as it's used for calculation.

caption: Examples of abnormalities of axons and myelin sheaths of the contralesional dorsal corticospinal tract in the spinal cord at the C5 level post-ICH in adult mice detected by transmission electron microscopy. High-magnification electron micrographs (3,900×) of contralesional corticospinal tract. (i) Normal axons with intact myelin sheaths in the sham group at W1. (ii) Dark degeneration of axons (white arrowhead) and abnormal myelin sheaths (black arrows) at W1 post-ICH. (iii) Accumulation of membranous debris within the axoplasm (white star), degenerated axon wrapped by loose myelin sheath lamellae (black asterisks), and infolding of myelin sheath (black star) at W2 post-ICH. (iv) Demyelinated axons (white asterisks) at W3 post-ICH. (v) Debris from the myelin sheath surrounding the shrunken axons (white arrows) at W4 post-ICH. (vi) The pathologies persisted at W5 post-ICH
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is transmission electron microscopy.
A2: This image depicts the contralesional dorsal corticospinal tract in the spinal cord at the C5 level.

A3: Yes, there are numerous abnormalities identified in this image, including dark degeneration of axons, abnormal myelin sheaths, accumulation of membranous debris within the axoplasm, degenerated axon wrapped by loose myelin sheath lamellae, infolding of myelin sheath, demyelinated axons, and debris from the myelin sheath surrounding the shrunken axons.
A4: This image shows significant irregularities, primarily within the axons and myelin sheaths after a post-ICH period.

A5: Yes, the image contains several significant labels indicating different pathologies at different time points (W1, W2, W3, W4, W5) post-ICH, including normal axons, dark degeneration of axons, accumulation of membranous debris, degenerated axon wrapped by loose myelin sheath, infolded myelin sheath, demyelinated axons, and debris surrounding shrunken axons.
